LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION. GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 815; VAN KEMPER V. HAYWARD AREA PARK DIST. (1979) 100 CAL. APP. 3D 822, 825. IF, IN THE COURSE OF DISCOVERY, FACTS EMERGE RELATED TO THAT CLAIM, PLAINTIFF MAY SEEK LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED A CAUSE OF ACTION, THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION, FOR PREMISES LIABILITY UNDER GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 835, A DANGEROUS CONDITION OF PUBLIC PROPERTY. THE DEMURRER TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. THE DEMURRER TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND WITHOUT OPPOSITION FROM PLAINTIFF. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-04-2008 Continued To Dec-29-08 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-06-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James Hannawalt of the Office of the City Attorney (554.3913) for defendant and moving party
Francis Shehadeh of the Dolan Law Firm (421.2800) for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the negligence cause of action. Government Code section 815; Van Kemper v. Hayward Area Park Dist. (1979) 100 Cal. App. 3d 822, 825. If, in the course of discovery, facts emerge related to that claim, plaintiff may seek leave to amend. Plaintiff has alleged a cause of action, the 3rd cause of action, for premises liability under Government Code section 835, a dangerous condition of public property. The demurrer to the 3rd cause of action is overruled. The demurrer to the 2nd cause of action is sustained without leave to amend without opposition from plaintiff. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
